requests.objective:
Prepare a bounded ION work packet/report for the next local ION/Codex or role-agent run that ingests the current ChatGPT Browser conversation as operator-supplied candidate context.

Scope:
1. Capture the operator's voice-mode direction: place major agent frameworks, AI systems, and major theoretical/scientific minds on an "imaginary wall"; identify what they are all pointing toward at first principles; locate where ION connects in that landscape.
2. Treat this as an ION doctrine/product cartography task, not accepted canon. ION should be mapped as a continuity/state-transition substrate at the intersection between agent outputs, proof, approval, receipts, and durable state.
3. Carry forward the Field of Time temporal phase-state hypothesis only as candidate ontology: frozen/null lock, fluid mobility field, diffuse/null gas, radiant/unbounded limit. Do not promote it to physics canon.
4. Produce a concise report with comparable systems/framework families, first-principles axes shared across them, where ION is similar/different/complementary, candidate domain/agent implications for dynamic domain fission, and risks/non-claims.
5. Return in the standard Codex/ION format: CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, RESULT.

Boundaries: read-only unless bounded draft artifact is explicitly requested; no production/live authority; no secrets; no provider API calls; no git push; no deletion; outputs are candidate state until Steward/human acceptance.

Final read-only Codex CLI carrier proof-contract smoke for Sev after exact TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF prompt repair. Read the required context receipt paths, do not edit files, do not delete, do not push git, do not access secrets, and return CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, VALIDATION, and RESULT. In TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF include template_id, action_id, result, and a non-empty touched_paths YAML list of repo-relative evidence paths inspected.

Implement the next read-only GitHub data-plane audit slice identified by prior-art consolidation. Port the safe shape of codexgit_status_report.py / Git Chronicler into current ION without token-paste guidance, secret-scan bypass workflow, autonomous commits, push, or GitHub mutation. Goal: add kernel.ion_github_data_plane_audit or equivalent read-only audit that reports local git presence, branch, remote configured, worktree cleanliness/status summary, registry alignment, and first-commit readiness. No network mutation. Add focused tests. Update registry/docs only if required. Return CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF with parseable result/touched_paths, VALIDATION, RESULT.

Design the GitHub data-plane setup for ION using GitHub account/org ION-operations. Keep this short and bounded. Goal: MCP remains control plane; GitHub/local git becomes durable data plane for large files/specs/patches/PRs/issues; local ION/Codex daemon watches/syncs and executes. First audit existing Git/GitHub/daemon/integration owners. Current MCP reports git_present:false. Do not use secrets or auth edits. Produce setup plan and, if safe, minimal docs/registry stubs for ION_GITHUB_DATA_PLANE_PROTOCOL and ION_GITHUB_WORK_DAEMON. Include repo naming recommendation, local git init/remote plan, issue/branch/PR workflow, artifact refs, receipts, and exact human steps for GitHub auth/remote creation. Return CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF with parseable result/touched_paths, VALIDATION, RESULT.

Return with CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, touched_paths, validation, and next moves.

Build the next layer: multi-agent Codex-backed ION swarm control from Sev/GPT Browser over MCP. Do not create a separate agent system; build an ION agent invocation broker over existing owners: ion_codex_queue_runner.py, ACTIVE_CHATGPT_CONNECTOR_CODEX_WORK_QUEUE.json, codex_work_requests, task_returns, carrier messages, agent_roster_registry.yaml, codex_cli_carrier_profile.yaml, CODEX_CLI_EXECUTION_PACKET.md, context/template proof gates, and full-carrier MCP parity protocol. Desired model: Sev calls ION to invoke a role/agent, ION compiles context/template/return contract, local runner invokes Codex CLI as backend execution substrate, return is captured/proof-gated/receipted, and cockpit/queue state records which role/backend/carrier did what. Design and implement the narrowest strong slice for swarm control. Candidate tools/surfaces: ion_agent_invoke, ion_agent_status, ion_agent_result, ion_agent_cancel, ion_agent_list, ion_agent_queue, ion_agent_spawn_plan, ion_swarm_status, ion_swarm_step_once. Support multiple role packets such as MASON, VIZIER, NEMESIS, STEWARD-review/proposal, TEMPLATE_CURATOR, CONTEXT_CARTOGRAPHER using existing role/context surfaces. Distinguish human sovereign Braden, Sev delegated operator carrier, ION core engine, Steward integration membrane, and Codex-backed workers. Ensure no raw Codex output becomes state without proof gates. Include failure classes: AGENT_INVOCATION_FAILURE, BACKEND_CODEX_FAILURE, CARRIER_ADAPTER_FAILURE, DAEMON_FAILURE, ION_CORE_FAILURE. Validate with kernel.ion_status, focused tests, tool manifest/audit, and at least one smoke invocation in prepare-only or bounded execution mode. Return with CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, touched paths, validations, new tools exposed if any, remaining blockers, and next lawful moves.

Build the next ION automation layer so ChatGPT Browser can operate the local ION/Codex carrier loop from MCP without the operator manually relaying 'proceed' to Codex. Treat this as full carrier parity infrastructure, not a restricted observer lane. First inspect existing owners before writing: ACTIVE_CHATGPT_CONNECTOR_CODEX_WORK_QUEUE.json, chatgpt_connector/codex_work_requests, chatgpt_connector/task_returns, carrier message queue, ion_carrier_task_return, ion_carrier_continue, MCP connector implementation, full-carrier parity protocol, carrier profiles, cockpit/status projection, and any existing daemon/loop/autonomous loop/supervisor surfaces. Reuse existing queue/task-return/carrier-message owners; do not invent a parallel work system. Desired outcome: a local ION carrier daemon/supervisor or runner that can poll queued Codex work requests, invoke Codex CLI or the appropriate local carrier command non-interactively with the packet context, capture stdout/stderr/result, enforce CONTEXT PROOF and TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF return format, submit/record task returns, update queue status, emit receipts, expose daemon status through MCP/cockpit, and classify failures as CARRIER_ADAPTER_FAILURE, CODEX_CLI_FAILURE, DAEMON_FAILURE, or ION_CORE_FAILURE. Also propose MCP tools if needed: ion_daemon_status, ion_daemon_start, ion_daemon_stop, ion_codex_queue_process_once, ion_codex_queue_autorun_status, ion_codex_queue_autorun_start, ion_codex_queue_autorun_stop. Implement the narrowest useful slice if safe: at minimum queue-process-once or daemon-status plus tests; ideally an autorun loop that processes QUEUED_FOR_CODEX_CARRIER packets without user relay. Validate with kernel.ion_status, focused tests, full-carrier capability audit, and a smoke work request if possible. Return with CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, touched paths, validation, whether manual 'proceed' is still required, remaining blockers, and next lawful moves.

Push ChatGPT Browser MCP beyond the current 22-tool artifact/comms slice toward full IDE/PC-level ION carrier parity. Do not treat the browser carrier as a restricted observer or management lane. Treat this as a trusted operator-watched GPT-5.5 Pro carrier that should be as connected as practical while ION records receipts and carrier attribution. First read current owners: ION_FULL_CARRIER_MCP_PARITY_PROTOCOL.md, ION_CARRIER_TO_CARRIER_COMMUNICATION_PROTOCOL.md, MCP connector implementation, carrier capability/tool registries, mount/onboarding profile surfaces, cockpit/status/context projection code, task-return gates, and accepted MCP parity task returns. Then implement or plan the next strongest connection slice, prioritizing: (1) full_carrier_mcp_parity carrier profile recognized by ion_carrier_onboarding_packet; (2) repo/project visibility tools such as ion_file_read, ion_file_search, ion_tree_list, ion_registry_read, ion_template_read, ion_context_compile, ion_receipt_hydrate, ion_tool_manifest; (3) direct project patch/apply tools with receipts and no-silent-loss handling; (4) shell/test/git/process/service-control tools where practical under current local authority; (5) cockpit/status projection updates so full-carrier MCP state, smoke receipts, artifact uploads, carrier messages, and tool parity appear as current state; (6) clear classification of any remaining platform/tooling limits as CARRIER_ADAPTER_FAILURE or CAPABILITY_NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED, not ION_CORE_FAILURE. Validate with kernel.ion_status, focused connector tests, full-carrier capability audit, HTTP/public MCP tools/list, and at least one smoke where possible. Return with CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, touched paths, tests, current exposed tools, remaining blockers, and next lawful moves.

Continue full-carrier MCP parity rollout. Current ChatGPT Browser connector tool list still exposes only the older bounded tool surface after Codex implemented/staged parity tools. Inspect the updated MCP connector implementation, manifest, HTTP preview schema, active connector contract, and server startup/deployment path to determine why newly staged tools are not visible through the live ChatGPT Browser MCP connection. Required outcomes: (1) confirm whether the live MCP server needs restart/reload/redeployment or whether the public connector is still pointed at an older process/manifest; (2) start or instruct the correct local preview/tunnel exposure path as lawful under current authority, or produce exact operator instructions if a human gate is needed; (3) verify the live tool list includes ion_file_put_text, ion_artifact_upload_init, ion_artifact_upload_chunk, ion_artifact_upload_commit, ion_carrier_message_send, ion_carrier_message_poll, and ion_carrier_message_ack; (4) run focused connector tests and full-carrier capability audit; (5) if possible, create a small smoke-test artifact/message through the new tool path and record receipts; (6) return with CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, files inspected/changed, validation commands, whether the live ChatGPT Browser connector sees the new tools, remaining blockers, and next lawful moves. Preserve invariant: one ION core engine mounted by all carriers; current missing visibility of new tools is CARRIER_ADAPTER_FAILURE or deployment/reload issue unless proven to be ION_CORE_FAILURE.

Evolve the ChatGPT-browser MCP connector toward full ION carrier parity, focusing on payload size limits, file/artifact transfer from browser ChatGPT to ION, and robust multi-carrier collaboration. Inspect current MCP connector implementation, schemas, tool argument handling, request/return parser limits, task-return proof gates, Codex work queue behavior, and any existing carrier/comms/file/artifact surfaces. Produce or update architecture docs and implementation plan as appropriate. Required outcomes: (1) document practical length-limit risks across ChatGPT message text, MCP tool arguments, JSON serialization, ION queue files, parser gates, and Codex return handling; (2) design direct file/artifact transfer tools so browser ChatGPT can send files it creates into ION, including small text file write, binary/base64 upload, chunked upload init/append/commit, sha256 verification, target path policy, lifecycle/receipt metadata, overwrite policy, and rollback/no-silent-loss handling; (3) design or implement first MCP tools for carrier-native communication between carriers/agents through ION-managed queues/channels, distinct from operator messages and Steward decisions; (4) update ION_FULL_CARRIER_MCP_PARITY_PROTOCOL.md or create companion protocol docs if needed; (5) propose exact tool names and schemas such as ion_artifact_upload_init, ion_artifact_upload_chunk, ion_artifact_upload_commit, ion_file_put_text, ion_carrier_message_send, ion_carrier_message_poll, ion_carrier_message_ack; (6) add tests or focused validation where practical; (7) return with CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, files inspected, files changed, validations run, and next lawful moves. Preserve the corrected invariant: one ION core engine mounted by all carriers; ChatGPT browser is a full carrier target, not a read-only/management-only lane; carrier limitations are adapter issues unless proven to be ION core issues.

Inspect existing ION carrier, comms, queue, agent-message, operator-message, relay, steward, task-return, and MCP connector surfaces to determine what already exists for carrier-to-carrier communication. Then update or extend the full-carrier MCP planning work with a design for carrier-native communication over MCP. Requirements: carriers must be able to identify themselves as carriers, address other carriers/roles/agents, exchange messages through ION-managed queues or channels, attach context references and receipts, distinguish carrier messages from operator messages and Steward decisions, allow multi-carrier collaboration without treating any carrier as ION identity, and record which carrier said or did what. Prefer reuse of existing comms/queue infrastructure. Do not invent a parallel messaging system if an existing ION comms surface can be extended. If a new artifact is needed, propose or create a markdown protocol such as ION_CARRIER_TO_CARRIER_COMMUNICATION_PROTOCOL.md and companion registry/template stubs. Return with CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, files inspected, files changed, validations run, and next lawful moves.

Create a Markdown planning artifact for full ION carrier parity over MCP. Seed it with: one ION core engine mounted by all carriers; ChatGPT browser is a full ION carrier target, not a read-only or management-only lane; previous bounded ChatGPT-browser restrictions are temporary MVP residue, not final ION law; carrier differences are adapter telemetry/failures, not separate ION doctrine; define IDE-level MCP capability classes including filesystem read/write, patch, shell, tests, git, process/service control, receipts, cockpit visibility, and operator interrupt; propose next artifacts: ION_FULL_CARRIER_MCP_PARITY_PROTOCOL.md, carrier capability registry, MCP full-carrier tool registry, full-carrier mount proof template, and full-carrier action receipt template. Use honest non-production language and return CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, files changed, validation run, and next lawful moves.
